content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 16, 1986

Barbara Boehm
City of Long Beach
City Hall/Plaza Level
333 W. Ocean Blvd.
Long Beach, CA 90802
PPI: Telephone Advice
No. I-86-165

Dear Barbara:

Thank you for returning my telephone call and providing me
with the information that I requested. This letter is a
follow-up to my earlier telephone conversation with Steve Ehren,
of your office, and our conversation about the new Conflict of
Interest filing requirements for city attorneys.

Steve Ehren indicated that Mr. John R. Calhoun, your new
city attorney, was appointed in October 1985 and that the
position is designated in your city's conflict of interest code.

In addition, Mr. Ehren advised me that the election for
city attorney was to be April 8, 1986. The newly appointed city
attorney filed his declaration of candidacy, including the
Candidate's Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721).
However, he was unopposed and therefore was appointed on
February 18, 1986. I advised Mr. Ehren that Mr. Calhoun would
be required to file an Assuming Office Statement of Economic
Interests (Form 721) pursuant to Gov. Code Section 87200
(amended by Stats 1985, Ch. 611, effective January 1, 1986).

Under the new law, city attorneys will be required to file
their first annual Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721)
with the Commission between January 1, 1987 and April 7, 1987.
Because of the unusual circumstances in this case, Mr. Calhoun's
annual statement should cover the period from January 1, 1986
(the effective date of the legislative change) through December
31, 1986.

If you have any additional questions, please call me at
(916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Alice Hughes
Technical Assistance and Analysis

AH:cab

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: What are the conflict of interest filing requirements for a newly appointed city attorney who was initially a candidate but was appointed unopposed before the scheduled election?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The newly appointed city attorney must file an Assuming Office Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721) pursuant to Government Code Section 87200, and his first annual statement should cover January 1, 1986 through December 31, 1986, with subsequent annual filings due between January 1 and April 7 each year.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a follow-up to telephone conversation; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers, but contains clear legal advice regarding filing requirements under Government Code Section 87200; letter references amended statute (Stats 1985, Ch. 611) effective January 1, 1986

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the conflict of interest filing requirements for a newly appointed city attorney who was initially a candidate but was appointed unopposed before the scheduled election?

CONCLUSION: The newly appointed city attorney must file an Assuming Office Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721) pursuant to Government Code Section 87200, and his first annual statement should cover January 1, 1986 through December 31, 1986, with subsequent annual filings due between January 1 and April 7 each year.

embedding.summary: FPPC technical assistance letter to City of Long Beach regarding conflict of interest filing requirements for newly appointed city attorney John R. Calhoun, clarifying that he must file an Assuming Office Statement and that his first annual statement should cover the period from the effective date of amended law through year-end 1986.
